title: podman pulls image "mongo" from bds-docker-release.jfrog.io
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue
Hell , i've just wanted to install an docker-composed Dockerfile with podman but stucked while pull the image "mongo:6.0" , but always getting the error "Errods-docker-release.jfrog.io/mongo:6.0"

I could explain why i uses "bds-docker-release.jfrog.io" and not "docker.io" , which works to pull, until I found out that the URL is hard written in /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/001-rhel-shortnames.conf . 

I can't say if this conf is from a local configuration or if it is from the podman package. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman pull mongo
2. vim /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/001-rhel-shortnames.conf (remove entry)
3. *works*


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

comments.body: 001-rhel-shortnames.conf is not something upstream uses and is RHEL specific so you have to report the issue to RHEL.

The alias is shipped with the short-names on RHEL; just tested it.  I do not know the reasoning behind but will catch up on it.
As @Luap99 mentioned, there is nothing that can be done upstream, so I encourage to go through the Red Hat channels or reach out to the Alma folks.
